name: Out of the mouth of babes
notes: Matthew anchors two scenes in the Psalms. At Matthew 13:35 the parabolic teaching of Jesus is declared a fulfilment of Psalm 78:2, the Asaph psalm that opens with a resolve to speak in a parable and utter dark sayings of old; the shared hook is 'I will open my mouth.' At Matthew 21:16, when the children cry out in the temple, Jesus answers the chief priests by citing Psalm 8:2, where praise rises out of the mouth of babes and sucklings. Where the Hebrew of Psalm 8:2 reads 'ordained strength,' Matthew follows the rendering 'perfected praise' (as in the Greek), a complementary rendering left as a matter for further study. Each side keeps its own vantage: the Psalms supply the prophetic word, and Matthew shows its appointed fulfilment in the ministry of Christ.

links.label: I will open my mouth ... I will utter
Out of the mouth of babes and sucklings
